title: Trizs, the fruitful village
description: The MOVING reference member state of this VC (Value Chain) is HUNGARY. The reference MOVING Card ID of this VC is VC 05 HU. In the framework of a local social employment programme (from 2010), poor local community members cultivate a three hectar community garden, producing fruit, vegetables, herbs and spices, complemented with forest fruits collected at the edge of the local national park. Processed on traditional ways into conserves, jams, compotes, etc. The programme provides employment for disadvantaged people, uses cropland on a traditional, but environmentally friendly way and provides healthy food products to urban customers through short food chains.

title: Mountain landscape and reference chains
description: The reference mountain chain of this VC is Northern Medium Mountains. The reference mountain landscape of this VC is Trizs. Trizs is a village in Borsod-Abaúj-Zemplén County, Putnok District. It is a settlement located in the northwestern part of the county, next to the Slovakian border and next to the Aggtelek National Park. The closest bigger city Miskolc is 50 km away and Budapest is 230 km away.

title: Interest on this VC
description: This is a 'public employment program' of the municipality of Trizs, perceived as a social economy based business of the village. Most of the people working in the program are women, which initially defined the scope of activities as well. The Women of Trizs bring their fruit and vegetable processing experiences from their own households to the community scene. They create high value added products and market it through short chains. The municipality initiated this project, which is not common, a high level of trust and co-operative culture has been developed in order to achieve both social and economic goals in the context of sustainable production. They sell the projects in their own local product shop, on the shelves of local, regional and more distant sample shops, and by other partners.

title: Innovation
description: The type of innovation of this VC (Value Chain) is Endogenous. The production and processing of fruit is traditional. However, using up-to-date design and packaging, selling the products through short food chains, using ICT and approaching CSA groups is innovative. The products are based on the knowledge of local people and the local municipality launched the initiative, using available assets (land, knowledge, funds for employment, investment, etc.). The innovation is related to New marketing strategies and New governance systems. The land use systems involved are The raw material is collected from the fruit trees of the region and from berry plantations. The local assets include Natural, social and cultural.
